spec.purpose: Add a `mint` variant and a `pill` size to the existing cva-based Button so the CourseCard CTA can be rendered without a one-off component.
changes: Add `mint` to the `variant` discriminated union in the cva config. Classes: `bg-brand-shade text-text-secondary [a]:hover:bg-brand-shade/80 hover:bg-brand-shade/80 active:scale-[0.98] transition-[transform,filter] duration-150`.
Add `pill` to the `size` discriminated union. Classes: `rounded-full px-6 h-11` (matches Figma 241x44 — width is content-driven via px-6).
Both new variant + size must compose with existing variants (so consumers can still use e.g. `variant='outline' size='pill'` without breakage).

acceptance: `buttonVariants` exports include `mint` variant and `pill` size — TypeScript-level (literal union widens).
Rendering `<Button variant="mint" size="pill">Book spot now</Button>` produces a 44px-tall pill with `#8EE0CE` background and `#2A2420` text — verified via class list.
No existing Button consumer in the repo regresses (grep for `<Button` and confirm prop shapes still type-check).
Uses tokens from DESIGN.md only — no raw hex in the new variant/size.

spec.purpose: Optionally add a `tone: 'default' | 'mint'` prop to CourseMetaChip so feature tiles can render with a mint tint when the Figma frame calls for it. Builder must first diff the Figma render (`_workspace/01_figma_context_node-1-3978*` assets / screenshots) — if tiles are pure white, downgrade this task to no-op with a `note` in the build log.
changes: When tone='mint', swap container classes from `bg-surface-1 border-border-1` to `bg-brand-lite border-border-2` (or `bg-mint-frost` if the Figma render reads more saturated). Builder picks the closer of the two existing tokens.
Do NOT introduce a new color token. Keep all other tile styling (size 110x104, gap-2, text-mini label, icon slot) intact.

acceptance: Prop `tone` is optional and defaults to 'default' — existing CourseMetaChip consumers (CourseTrayInline, etc.) compile unchanged.
With tone='mint' the container background resolves to `bg-brand-lite` OR `bg-mint-frost` (builder's call after Figma diff) — no raw hex.
If Figma diff shows pure white tiles: file remains unchanged, build log records the decision under T2 as `no-op`, and T3 must omit the `tone` prop.
Tile size (110x104), radius, label typography (`text-mini text-text-secondary`), and icon slot are unchanged across both tones.

spec.purpose: Horizontal 1308x362 course card. Left half (447x362) is a hero photo; right half is a column with eyebrow caption, title, body, 4-tile feature row, price (top-right), mint CTA, and a secondary 'Starts in ...' caption (bottom-right).
layout_notes: Outer surface: `relative flex w-[1308px] h-[362px] overflow-hidden rounded-lg bg-surface-1 border border-border-1`.
Left photo block: `relative w-[447px] h-[362px] shrink-0` containing next/image with `fill` and `object-cover`.
Right column: `flex-1 pl-10 pr-10 py-6 flex flex-col` (40px left gutter from image edge).
Title at y=26, description at y=84, feature tile row at y=160 (h=104, 4 tiles x 110px + 3 gaps x 15px = 485px), CTA at y=289.
Price absolutely positioned at top-right (`absolute top-6 right-10`), startsCaption at bottom-right (`absolute bottom-6 right-10`).
decisions_applied: CourseCard root is NOT a Link. Card surface is decorative — no whole-card navigation (resolves the click-conflict in 02_interactions.json).
CTA href = '#' placeholder (real /courses/[slug]/book route does not exist yet).
Builder must surface `unresolved hrefs` (cta) in the build log so visual-qa / orchestrator can backfill.

actions.unresolved_reason: Real booking route /courses/[slug]/book not yet implemented — using '#' placeholder per user decision.

actions.note: Already included in T1's new mint variant classes.

reduced_motion.rationale: Read `prefers-reduced-motion` via framer-motion's `useReducedMotion()` hook. If true, skip the variants (set initial='visible' or pass `transition={{ duration: 0 }}`) so the card renders statically. Demo page may additionally wrap in `<MotionConfig reducedMotion='user'>` for belt-and-braces.

implementation_notes: Use `motion.article` for root with `variants={containerVariants}` and `initial='hidden' whileInView='visible' viewport={{ once: true, amount: 0.3 }}`.

Each animated child (image wrapper, title, body, feature-row, price, cta, starts-caption) is a `motion.div` (or motion(Link) for cta if next/link is used) with shared `itemVariants` and individual `transition={{ delay: <ms> }}`.
Hover-lift remains Tailwind-only on the root; does not conflict with framer-motion's opacity+y animation.
Card root is NOT clickable — only the CTA navigates. No nested-anchor risk.

acceptance: Renders a 1308x362 horizontal card with a 447px left photo and a right content column; title in `text-h5`, body in body/subtext tokens, price in `text-text-brand`.

Feature tile row renders 4 CourseMetaChip instances with the Figma icons + labels (calendar/Sat & Sun, yoga-pose/Studio, clock/3 days, english/English). Tile tone matches T2's decision (mint or default).
CTA renders `<Button variant="mint" size="pill">` and links to `href="#"` via next/link (or an `<a>`). Build log records this as an unresolved href.

Mount stagger animation: root uses framer-motion `whileInView` with `viewport={{ once: true, amount: 0.3 }}`; children fade-in-up in the order image(0) → title(60ms) → body(120ms) → feature-row/price(180ms) → cta(240ms) → caption(300ms).
Root carries Tailwind hover-lift classes (`transition-all duration-300 ease-out hover:-translate-y-1 hover:shadow-md`) and the card surface itself is NOT clickable — only the CTA navigates.
Respects `prefers-reduced-motion`: uses `useReducedMotion()` to skip the entrance animation (or MotionConfig wrapper) — verifiable by toggling the OS setting or DevTools emulation.

spec.purpose: Renders CourseCard with the exact Figma content (node 1:3978) so visual-qa can screenshot and diff against the Figma reference.
imports: Lucide icons (Calendar, Clock) and/or local SVGs for yoga-pose + english

image.alt: Group of students practicing yoga with a teacher guiding inversions

content.title: Decoding "What is Prana?"
content.description: Pay once. Access 25 expert-led video lessons at your own pace, forever. From beginner inversions to advanced flows.

spec.page_chrome: Center the card on a neutral `bg-surface-2` background with `min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center p-12`. No header/footer — keep the screenshot focused on the card.

acceptance: Visiting `/demo/course-card` renders exactly one CourseCard at its native 1308x362 footprint, with all Figma content populated.
Asset `/course-prana.png` is present in `apps/web/public/` (builder copies/places from the Figma export) and loads via next/image without 404.
Page is wrapped in `<MotionConfig reducedMotion="user">` (or relies on CourseCard's `useReducedMotion()` hook) — reduced-motion users see a static render.
Page has no other interactive controls — the CTA is the only navigable element and points to `#`.

demo_page.purpose: Render the CourseCard with Figma content for visual-qa screenshot diff.

unresolved_hrefs.reason: User decision: pages don't exist yet — use '#' placeholder. Real route TBD (likely `/courses/[slug]/book`).
